policy_statement: Chile will keep its GHG emissions budget to no more than 1,100 MtCO2eq for 2020–2030, with emissions peaking by 2025 and reaching 95 MtCO2eq by 2030 (M1).

In 2020, Chile will establish a public-private dialogue roundtable to define policy for the use of markets under Article 6, with guidelines to preserve environmental integrity, avoid double-counting, and promote sustainable development (M1).

Strengthen national coordination on adaptation through the national adaptation plan and adaptation plans for 11 priority sectors, using them as inputs to an Adaptation Communication to the UNFCCC by 2022 at the latest (A2).
Update the National Adaptation Plan by 2022 and begin its implementation (A2a).
Develop initial adaptation plans for water resources, coastal areas, and the mining sector by 2022 and begin implementation (A2b).

Update and implement sectoral adaptation plans on the stated 2021–2028 schedule (including updates for Agriculture and Forestry, Biodiversity, Fisheries and Aquaculture, Health, Facilities, Energy, Cities, Tourism, Water Resources, Coastal Areas, and Mining) (A2c).

By 2025, strengthen subnational climate capacities and institutionality and begin implementing adaptation/mitigation via Regional Climate Change Action Plans in 10 regions; by 2030, all 16 regions will have climate action plans (A3).
Update and expand climate vulnerability and risk studies during NDC implementation, considering gender, to inform adaptation measure design (A4).
By 2021, develop a communal-level climate risk map platform for Chile's continental territory (A4a).
By 2021, estimate the costs of inaction on climate change; by 2025, estimate costs associated with historic losses and damage (A4b).
By 2025, assess nationwide climate change risk to vulnerable groups, focusing on indigenous peoples, poverty, and gender (A4c).
By 2026, strengthen the monitoring and evaluation system with progress and impact indicators for all climate change adaptation instruments (A5).
Strengthen inclusion of non-governmental actors in planning and implementing adaptation measures during the NDC implementation period (A6).
By 2025, implement and keep updated a record of adaptation actions by non-governmental actors (A6a).
By 2030, establish public-private cooperation mechanisms to execute adaptation actions at national and local scale (A6b).
Enhance information mechanisms to manage climate change impacts on water resources to improve resilience (A7).
By 2025, establish a national indicator for hydrological watersheds to track water shortages and risk (A7a).
By 2025, create a system to certify water consumption management levels at an organizational level through the HuellaChile Program (A7b).
By 2030, establish Watershed Strategic Plans for Water Resources Management in Chile’s 101 hydrological basins, incorporating climate change adaptation (A7c).
By 2030, inspect and register 95% of Rural Health Systems to ensure rural drinking water quality standards (A7d).
By 2030, ensure 100% of companies in the health sector implement disaster risk management plans that consider climate-change-related risks (A7e).
By 2030, implement 100% of the 2030 Agenda goals for the health sector (A7f).

By 2030, require all water-related public infrastructure projects to evaluate protection of population/land (e.g., river works) and prioritize urban/rural human water consumption demands in their areas of influence (A7g).
By 2030, reduce non-treated sewage by at least 25% (A7h).
Strengthen nationwide capacity to adapt to climate-related risks and manage adverse effects of socio-natural disasters (A8).
By 2021, develop guidelines on climate change effects on human mobility in Chile aligned with international instruments (A8a).
By 2022, develop a specific national plan for heat-wave-associated risk variables via cross-sectoral work of the Meteorological Risk Variables Working Group (A8b).
By 2025, all regions will incorporate climate change adaptation actions into Regional Disaster Risk Reduction Plans in coordination with CORECC (A8c).
By 2030, fully implement the National Policy for Disaster Risk Reduction 2019–2030, harmonize it with Sendai Framework, Paris Agreement and 2030 Agenda, and review it by 2023 (A8d).

policy_statement: Establish new protected areas in under-represented marine ecoregions and in coastal wetland ecosystems to supplement the marine network (I10).

By 2030, protect at least 10% of under-represented marine ecoregions (Humboldt, Central Chile, Araucanía and Chiloe) via participatory, science-based marine spatial planning including climate-change criteria (I10a).
By 2025, protect at least 20 coastal wetlands as new protected areas (I10b).
By 2030, protect at least 10 additional coastal wetlands as protected areas (I10c).
Ensure all marine protected areas created up to 2020 have management/administration plans under implementation that include climate change adaptation actions (I11).
By 2030, ensure 100% of MPAs created between 2020 and 2025 have management/administration plans including climate change adaptation actions (I11c).
By 2025, implement management/administration plans for at least 40% of MPAs created before 2020 through monitoring, control, community involvement, and threat control programs (I11b).
By 2030, implement a methodology to evaluate management effectiveness for 100% of MPA management/administration plans to track mitigation and adaptation targets (I11e).
Assess co-benefits of ecosystems in marine protected areas for mitigation and adaptation and implement actions to strengthen these co-benefits (I12).
By 2025, develop standardized metrics in three MPAs to evaluate their adaptation/mitigation capacities (I12a).
By 2030, apply monitoring and verification metrics in at least five MPAs and strengthen co-benefits in their management plans (I12b).
